ddls:
CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the customer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the customer */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the customer record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the customer record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "email_campaigns" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each email campaign */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the email campaign */,
  "subject" TEXT /* Subject of the email campaign */,
  "content" TEXT /* Content of the email campaign */,
  "sent_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the email campaign was sent */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the email campaign record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the email campaign record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_email_interactions" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each interaction */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "email_campaign_id" INTEGER /* ID of the email campaign */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the interaction */,
  "email_received" INTEGER /* Whether the email was received by the customer */,
  "email_opened" INTEGER /* Whether the email was opened by the customer */,
  "link_clicked" INTEGER /* Whether the link in the email was clicked by the customer */,
  "link_click_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the link was clicked by the customer */,
  "email_forwarded" INTEGER /* Whether the email was forwarded by the customer */,
  "email_replied" INTEGER /* Whether the customer replied to the email */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the interaction record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the interaction record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_segments" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer segment */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the customer segment */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the customer segment */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the customer segment record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the customer segment record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_segment_memberships" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer segment membership */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "customer_segment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer segment */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the customer segment membership record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the customer segment membership record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g. admin, analyst, campaign manager) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the permission record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the permission record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_permissions" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user permission */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* ID of the permission */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user permission record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user permission record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)
